purpose: COS.Lending.Selling.UI is a React-based frontend application for Cross River Bank's loan selling platform. It provides interfaces for loan management, tracking loan history, and facilitating loan transactions between different stakeholders in the lending ecosystem.
businessFeatures: LTHF (Loans To Be Held For Sale) management

apis.purpose: Retrieve HFS (Held For Sale) loans data
Create or retrieve a chat session for loan history queries
Retrieve chat history for a loan session
Fetch detailed information about a specific loan

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core entity representing a loan in the system with its attributes

dataEntities.name: LoanDetails
dataEntities.description: Extended details for a specific loan

dataEntities.name: PendingApproval
dataEntities.description: Represents loans awaiting approval in the workflow

dataEntities.name: ChatSession
dataEntities.description: Represents a conversation session for querying loan history

findings.title: Security token exposure in Cypress tests
findings.description: Cypress tests are capturing authentication tokens and storing them in localStorage. This practice can expose sensitive tokens in test logs and potentially lead to security breaches. Consider implementing a mock authentication mechanism for tests instead.

findings.title: CORS bypass configuration for development
findings.description: The README instructs users to disable CORS in Chrome for development, which is a security risk. A proper development environment should have correctly configured CORS headers. Consider implementing a proper local development proxy.

ambiguities: The relationship between loans and LTHFs (Loans To Be Held For Sale) is not clearly defined in the code
The full authentication flow and token management strategy is not completely visible from the frontend code
The specific business rules for loan approvals and the complete approval workflow are not fully documented in the code
The exact schema of loan entities and their relationships is not fully defined in the frontend code
The role of external parties (MPLs, Investors, Issuing Banks) and their specific interactions with the system
